bead.title: F (Foundation): Operation pattern + IDGenerator + sentinels + ctx access: part 1
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-c6317784 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 5 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
# PROBLEM
The bead backend interface today (`cli/internal/bead/backend.go`) is JSONL-shaped: `RawBackend` requires whole-corpus `ReadAll`/`WriteAll`, and `Backend` declares only 22 of `*Store`'s 69 public methods. Per TD-027 (the canonical Bead Storage System and Lifecycle spec), the bead architecture needs:

- A typed `Operation` pattern with `Apply(b *Bead) error` (CAS via error returns)
- 11 LSP-clean storage sub-interfaces composing `Backend`
- Pluggable `IDGenerator` and parallel `LifecycleSubscriber`
- `context.Context` first-parameter discipline on every interface method
- Sentinel errors (`ErrNotFound`, `ErrConflict`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrAlreadyClaimed`, `ErrNotClaimedByOwner`, `ErrUnsupported`)
- The optional `OperationApplier` interface on `RawBackend` so JSONL backends can preserve heartbeat sidecar optimization
- A canonical Bead data model (field enumeration + invariants + JSONL wire format + schema-versioning policy)

This bead lays the foundation that all per-concern beads (L/C/E/X/Q/D/A/MIG) depend on. It declares the event reader/writer interfaces but deliberately does not migrate `AppendEvent`, `Events`, or `EventsByKind`; the E bead owns event-method ctx threading and `*Store` event interface satisfaction.

# ROOT CAUSE
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go:10-67` — `RawBackend` (4 whole-corpus methods) + `Backend` (22 high-level methods, incomplete).
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `*Store` exposes 69 public methods, none take `ctx context.Context`.
- `cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go:340-381` — `WriteAll` ships entire corpus per mutation (catastrophic for Postgres).
- Operation pattern, IDGenerator, sentinel errors, context discipline — all absent.
- Bead data model (the canonical schema of fields + invariants) — only implicit in the Go struct.

# PROPOSED FIX
Implement TD-027 §5–§13 foundation pieces that are required before the per-concern beads can execute.

## New files

1. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` — `Operation` interface with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter for ad-hoc mutations. Empty named-op section (per-concern beads add their op types).
2. `cli/internal/bead/id.go` — `ValidateID`, format constants (`DefaultIDPrefix`, `MaxIDLength`, `MinIDLength`), `IDGenerator` interface, `RandomHexIDGenerator`, `SequentialIDGenerator`, `NewIDGenerator()` default.
3. `cli/internal/bead/errors.go` — sentinel e
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- All five new files (`operation.go`, `id.go`, `errors.go`, `context.go`, `backend.go` expansion) exist and compile.
- `Operation` interface declared with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter implements `Operation`. Test: `TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip`.
- ID generation: tests `TestValidateID_AcceptsValidIDs`, `TestValidateID_RejectsInvalidCharset`, `TestValidateID_RejectsLengthBounds`, `TestRandomHexIDGenerator_ProducesValidIDs` (100 generations all pass `ValidateID`), `TestSequentialIDGenerator_ProducesUniqueOrdered` (1000 generations all distinct + lexicographically increasing).

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. All five new files (`operation.go`, `id.go`, `errors.go`, `context.go`, `backend.go` expansion) exist and compile.
2. `Operation` interface declared with `Apply(b *Bead) error`. `MutateFunc` adapter implements `Operation`. Test: `TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip`.
3. ID generation: tests `TestValidateID_AcceptsValidIDs`, `TestValidateID_RejectsInvalidCharset`, `TestValidateID_RejectsLengthBounds`, `TestRandomHexIDGenerator_ProducesValidIDs` (100 generations all pass `ValidateID`), `TestSequentialIDGenerator_ProducesUniqueOrdered` (1000 generations all distinct + lexicographically increasing).

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"description drops commitment \"TestOperation_MutateFuncAdapter_RoundTrip\"","fingerprint":"9a9f3e7563c088c6559b50170a909615619d5996d3af04d242043c3656f19c85","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"rewrite_rejected","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.rewrite_rejected","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"rationale":"Description and label structure are present, but the title is not a clear imperative one-line scope, acceptance criteria omit named Test* symbols and the required `go test` / `lefthook` commands, there is no explicit deps field or `No deps.` statement, and the bead is not yet a fully sufficient sub-agent prompt.","score":2,"suggested_fixes":["Rewrite the title as an imperative, single-scope change that names the subsystem and action.","Move the specific `Test*` names into acceptance criteria and add the required `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./\u003cpkg\u003e/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` commands there.","Add an explicit deps list or state `No deps.` if none are required.","Add a wired assertion requirement for each introduced code path in the acceptance criteria.","If this slice still bundles multiple independent subsystems, split it into narrower child beads."],"waivers_applied":[]}
